info.name: Poly VideoOS REST API (v3.7.0) - Complete
info.description: Complete Postman collection generated from Poly VideoOS REST API Reference Guide Software 3.7.0 (September 2021 | 3725-86572-007A)

request.description:
This API provides details about the systems audio information.
Output: `{ "muteLocked": false, "muted": true, "numOfMicsConnected": 1, "volume": 62 }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs an audio action on the system related to pressed DTMF characters.
Actions: “start”, “playDTMF”, “playTestTone”, “stopPlayingTestTone”, “itpSegmentDetection”.
Speakers: “SPEAKER_LEFT”, “SPEAKER_RIGHT”, “SPEAKER_BOTH”.
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API performs acoustic fence actions and broadcasts audio fence indicators.
Actions: “start”, “playDTMF”, “playTestTone”, “stopPlayingTestTone”, “itpSegmentDetection”.
Speakers: “SPEAKER_LEFT”, “SPEAKER_RIGHT”, “SPEAKER_BOTH”.
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API provides system audio meter information.
Output: Array of audio meter objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the list of microphones with relevant information for each one.
Output: Array of microphone objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the microphone mute status.
Output: `<boolean>`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs an audio action on the system to mute or unmute microphones.
Input: `<boolean>` (True mutes, False unmutes)
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API provides information on the microphone volume level.
Output: `<integer>`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs an audio action on the system to set volume level.
Input: `<integer>`
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API provides the status of the meetings calendar server.
Output: `{ Status: "ONLINE" | "OFFLINE" | ... }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

query.description: (Optional) Max number of meetings. Cannot be combined with start/end.
(Optional) Start time (epoch seconds). Default: current time.
(Optional) End time (epoch seconds). Default: 24 hours after start.

request.description:
This API provides a list of meetings in the calendar. Query parameters may specify either a start and end time range, or a maximum number of upcoming events from the current time. The default query is to return all upcoming meetings within 24 hours from the current time.
Output: Array of meeting objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique meeting identifier to retrieve from the calendar.

request.description:
This API provides details for a single meeting in the calendar.
Output: Single meeting object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique meeting identifier to dial.

request.description:
This API dials the calendar meeting specified by meetingId.
Output: `{ "connectionId": ..., "success": true }`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API retrieves information about the calendar server based on the specified e-mail address and credentials.
Domain: "sip" or "email".
Output: `{ "error": "NOERROR", "serverUrl": "..." }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the selection availability of the near source camera.
Output: `true`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API provides the system content status.
Output: `"CONTENT_IDLE"`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the index number and information for all near camera sources.
Output: Array of camera objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the index number and information for all near people camera sources.
Output: Array of people camera objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the index number and information for all near content camera sources.
Output: Array of content camera objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier for the camera index.

request.description:
This API provides information for the near camera specified by sourceID.
Output: Single camera object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier for the camera index.

request.description:
This API performs the move operation for the near camera specified by sourceID.
Actions: “moveStart”, “moveStop”, “store”, “clear”, “activate”.
Directions: “MOVE_LEFT”, “MOVE_RIGHT”, “MOVE_UP”, “MOVE_DOWN”, “MOVE_ZOOMIN”, “MOVE_ZOOMOUT”, “MOVE_FOCUSNEAR”, “MOVE_FOCUSFAR”.
Messages: “LeftCamera”, “RightCamera”, “BothCamera”.
WithImage: “Yes”, “No”.
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API provides information for the selected near people camera source.
Output: Selected people camera object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs the move operation for the selected near people camera source.
(See 'Move Specific Near Camera' for body details)
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides information for the selected near content camera source.
Output: Selected content camera object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs the move operation for the selected near content camera source.
(See 'Move Specific Near Camera' for body details)
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the list of near camera presets.
Output: Array of preset objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the near camera preset index.

request.description:
This API provides the near camera presets information for a specified camera stored in the index.
Output: Single preset object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the near camera preset index.

request.description:
This API updates the near camera presets information for a specified camera stored in the index.
Actions: “moveStart”, “moveStop”, “store”, “clear”, “activate”.
(See 'Move Specific Near Camera' for other body details)
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The available value is SELECTED_PEOPLE.

request.description:
This API performs the move operation for the selected near camera source, using the exact given coordinates or the coordinates as an offset from the current position.
Pan/Tilt/Zoom range: -50000 to +50000.
Relative: True sets exact coordinates, False sets offset.
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API provides the list and information for all far camera sources.
Output: Array of far camera objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API provides information for the selected far people camera source.
Output: Selected far people camera object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs the move operation for the selected far people camera source.
(See 'Move Specific Near Camera' for body details)
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier for the camera index.

request.description:
This API provides information for the far camera specified by sourceID.
Output: Single camera object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier for the camera index.

request.description:
This API performs the move operation for the far camera specified by sourceID.
(See 'Move Specific Near Camera' for body details)
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

variable.description: Available values: near_image_1.jpg, far_image_1.jpg, or content_image_1.jpg.

request.description:
This API returns a stored snapshot specified by snapshotID.
Output: Image data (not JSON)
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API provides the current state of the collaboration session.
Output: `{ "SessionId": <string>, "sessionState": "ACTIVE" }`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API performs an action on the active collaboration session.
Action: "END".
Codes: 200 OK, 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

query.description: The room pin to authorize access to content session

request.description:
This API returns a context ID and begins the content save process.
Output: `{ "context_id": "<string>" }`
Codes: 200 OK, 204 No Content, 403 Forbidden, 500 Server Error

query.description: The room pin to authorize access to content session.
The context_id provided from the collaboration/content POST command.

request.description:
This API provides previously saved content as an attachment, using context_id from the collaboration/content POST command.
Output: File attachment
Codes: 200 OK, 204 No Content, 500 Server Error

query.description: The room pin to authorize access to content session.
True enables save. False disables save.

request.description:
This API provides the content save enable/disable status.
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

query.description: The room pin to authorize access to content session.

request.description:
This API provides and downloads a ZIP file containing saved content from the active collaboration session.
Output: ZIP file attachment
Codes: 200 OK, 204 No Content, 403 Forbidden, 500 Server Error

This API provides a list of all current conference calls on the system.
Output: Array of conference objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs a single participant conference call.
Dial Types: “LOOPBACK”, “AUTO”, “VIDEO”, “VOICE”, “H320”, “H323”, “SIP”, “GATEWAY”, “POTS”, “ISDN_VOICE”, “SIP_SPEAKERPHONE”, “POTS_SPEAKERPHONE”, “VOICE_H323”, “VOICE_SIP”, “VOICE_GATEWAY”.
Output: Array with link object `[ { "rel": "string", "href": "string" } ]`
Codes: 200 OK, 415 Error

This API performs a blast dial to establish a multiple participant conference call.
(See 'Start Single Participant Conference' for Dial Types)
Output: Link object `{ "rel": "string", "href": "string" }`
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 415 Error

This API provides the system’s conference call capabilities.
Output: `{ "canBlastDial": true, "canMakeAudioCall": true, "canMakeVideoCall": true }`
Codes: 200 OK

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference to retrieve.

request.description:
This API provides information for the specified conference call.
Output: Conference object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference to hang up.

request.description:
This API hangs up and disconnects the specified conference call.
Output: `0`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

query.description: Available values: ALL, ACTIVE, ANSWERABLE, INCOMING, PENDING.

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.

request.description:
This API provides a list of connections associated to the specified conference.
Output: Array of connection objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.

request.description:
This API dials and adds a new endpoint connection to an active conference specified by confID.
Output: Array with link object `[ { "rel": "self", "href": "string" } ]`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 415 Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.
The unique identifier for the connection.

request.description:
This API provides information for a specific connection using connID, on the specified conference call using confID.
Output: Connection object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.
The unique identifier for the connection to hang up.

request.description:
This API hangs up and disconnects a specific connection using connID, on the specified conference call using confID.
Output: `0`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.

request.description:
This API provides a list of terminals associated with the specified conference call using confID.
Output: Array of terminal objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.
The unique identifier for the terminal.

request.description:
This API provides a specific terminal using termID, associated with the specified conference call using confID.
Output: Terminal object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.
The unique identifier for the terminal to hang up.

request.description:
This API hangs up and disconnects a specific terminal using termID, associated with the specified conference call using confID.
Output: `0`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.

request.description:
This API provides a list of media statistics for connections associated to the specified conference.
Output: Array of media stat objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.
The unique identifier for the connection.

request.description:
This API provides media statistics for a specific connection using connID, on the specified conference call using confID.
Output: Array of media stat objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.
The unique identifier for the terminal.

request.description:
This API provides media statistics for a specific terminal using termID, associated with the specified conference call using confID.
Output: Array of media stat objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

variable.description: The unique identifier of the conference.

request.description:
This API provides quality metrics for all terminal endpoints associated with the specified conference call using confID.
Output: Array of quality objects `[ { "id": "string", "quality": 0 } ]`
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

This API provides a list of favorite directory entries, sorted by the favorite’s index value.
Output: Object with 'entries' array and 'next' link (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API provides information about the configured global directory server.
Output: Server info object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides information about the multi-tier directory group entry, if configured.
Output: Entry object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API provides information about the configured local directory server.
Output: Server info object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API adds a new entry to the local directory server.
Entry Type: "SINGLE", "GROUP".
Address Type: "IP_ADDRESS", "IP_EXTENSION", "H323_NAME", "SIP", "POTS", "ISDN", "EMAIL", "TELEPHONE", "MOBILE".
Codes: 201 Created, 400 Bad Request, 405 Not Allowed, 500 Server Error, 507 Insufficient Storage

variable.description: The unique identifier of the entry to retrieve.

request.description:
This API provides information about the specified local directory entry.
Output: Entry object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the entry to delete.

request.description:
This API removes an entry from the local directory server.
Codes: 204 No Content, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the entry to update.

request.description:
This API performs an update to an existing entry within the local directory server.
(See 'Add Local Directory Entry' for body details)
Codes: 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the entry.

request.description:
This API performs an action on the specified entry within the local directory server.
Action: "dial".
DeviceId: Optional device ID to dial.
Codes: 204 No Content, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the entry.

request.description:
This API performs an action to set or unset favorite status on an existing entry within the local directory server.
Input: `{ "favorite": <boolean> }`
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the favorite entry.

request.description:
This API performs an update to the position order of an existing favorite entry within the local directory server.
Input: `{ "index": <integer> }`
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the group entry.

request.description:
This API performs an action on the members of the specified directory group within the local directory server.
Actions: "query", "add", "remove".
EntryId: Optional entry ID for add/remove actions.
Codes: 201 Created, 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API performs a new asynchronous directory entries search query.
Server Scope: "ALL", "LOCAL", "GLOBAL", "FAVORITES".
Entry Scope: "SINGLE", "GROUP", "ANY_TIERED", "ANY".
Group Scope: "ONE_LEVEL", "RECURSIVE".
Order: "FIRST_NAME", "LAST_NAME", "DISPLAY_NAME".
Output: `{ "id": "<queryId>" }`
Codes: 201 Created, 500 Server Error

This API provides a new asynchronous directory entries search query, used to retrieve a default list of directory entries.
Output: `{ "id": "<queryId>" }`
Codes: 201 Created, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The query ID of the directory search to retrieve.

request.description:
This API provides results of a directory entries search for a specified query ID.
Output: Object with 'entries' array and 'next' link (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The query ID of the directory search to delete.

request.description:
This API removes a directory entry search query for a specified query ID.
Codes: 204 No Content, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the directory server (e.g., 'local', 'global').
The unique identifier of the entry to retrieve.

request.description:
This API provides a specified directory entry from the specified directory server.
Output: Entry object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the directory server.
The unique identifier of the entry.

request.description:
This API performs an action on the specified entry within the specified directory server.
Action: "dial".
DeviceId: Optional device ID to dial.
Codes: 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error, 501 Not Implemented, 507 Insufficient Storage

variable.description: The unique identifier of the directory server.
The unique identifier of the entry.

request.description:
This API performs an action to set or unset favorite status on the specified entry within the specified directory server.
Input: `{ "favorite": <boolean> }`
Output: `{ "success": true, "reason": "string" }`
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the directory server.
The unique identifier of the favorite entry.

request.description:
This API performs an update to the position order of the specified entry within the specified directory server.
Input: `{ "index": <integer> }`
Output: `{ "success": true, "reason": "string" }`
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

variable.description: The unique identifier of the directory server.
The unique identifier of the group entry.

request.description:
This API performs an action on the members of the specified directory group within the specified directory server.
Actions: "query", "add", "remove".
EntryId: Optional entry ID for add/remove actions.
Output: `{ "id": "<queryId>" }` (for query action)
Codes: 201 Created, 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 404 Unavailable, 500 Server Error

This API performs an import of an XML file containing directory entries.
Input: XML file content in the request body.
Codes: 204 No Content, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API performs an export of directory entries to an XML file and makes them available for download.
Output: XML file attachment.
Codes: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 500 Server Error

This API creates and authenticates a session on the system.
Output: Session details object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 SUCCESS, 403 LOG-IN ATTEMPT FAILED

This API provides the status and information of the H323 gatekeeper servers.
Output: Array of gatekeeper objects `[ { “state”: “ON”, “address”: “string”, “version”: “string”, “model”: “string” } ]`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the system logs in a .tar file as an octet stream file attachment response.
Output: .tar file attachment.
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API starts the Environment Control app on a paired touch controller. This API only supports one connected TC8 device.
Output: `{ "result": true }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API hides the Environment Control app on a paired touch controller. This API only supports one connected TC8 device.
Output: `{ "result": true }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API gets the status of the Environment Control app on a paired touch controller in the form of a list. This API only supports one connected TC8 device.
Output: `[ { "macAddress": "<string>", "environmentControlAppActive": true } ]`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the status of whether the Device Mode application is currently running.
Output: `{ “result”: true }`
Codes: 200 OK

This API starts Device Mode if it isn’t running.
Output: `{ "success”: true, “reason”: “string” }`
Codes: 200 OK

This API stops Device Mode if it is running.
Output: `{ "success”: true, “reason”: “string” }`
Codes: 200 OK

This API provides the status of whether the Digital Signage application is currently running.
Output: `{ “result”: true }`
Codes: 200 OK

This API starts Digital Signage if it isn’t running.
Output: `{ "success”: true, “reason”: “string” }`
Codes: 200 OK

This API stops Digital Signage if it is running.
Output: `{ "success”: true, “reason”: “string” }`
Codes: 200 OK

This API performs an ICMP echo ping to the destination network host.
Input: `{ "destination": <string> }`
Output: `{ "icmpReachable": true, "responseTime": 0, "h323Reachable": true, "sipReachable": true }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs network traceroute to the destination network host.
Input: `{ "destination": <string> }`
Output: Traceroute result object (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs a reboot of the system.
Input: `{ "action": "reboot" }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs a reset of configurations and settings of the system.
Input: `{ "action": "resetsettings", "keepCertificates": <boolean>, "keepCdr": <boolean>, "keepDirectory": <boolean>, "keepLogs": <boolean> }` (True retains, False resets)
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the status and information of the SIP servers.
Output: `{ “state”: “ON”, “address”: “string”, “version”: “string”, “model”: “string” }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the status and information of important areas of the system.
Output: Array of status objects (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the current time in milliseconds of the system, using UTC time zone and the time offset for the currently set time zone.
Output: `{ "time”: <milliseconds_since_epoch>, “tzOffset”: <offset_in_minutes> }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API performs a set of the current system time using Unix Epoch Time format (milliseconds).
Input: `{ "time": <milliseconds_since_epoch>, "tzOffset": <integer> (optional) }`
Codes: 200 OK

This API provides the current time in a single ISO 8601 formatted string value.
Output: `{ "time”: “YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ” }`
Codes: 200 OK, 500 Server Error

This API provides the media status value(s) associated with all shared content.
Output: Object with 'vars' array (see PDF for example).
Codes: 200 OK

This API provides the status of whether the local video is hidden (muted).
Output: `{ “result”: true }` (true if hidden/muted)
Codes: 200 OK

This API performs a hide (mute) or unhide (unmute) action on the local video.
Input: `{ "mute”: <boolean> }` (True hides, False unhides)
Output: `{ "success”: true, “reason”: “string” }`
Codes: 200 OK

variable.description: The conference ID to check.

request.description:
This API provides the status of whether the remote video is hidden (muted) on a specified conference.
Output: `{ “result”: true }` (true if hidden/muted)
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable

item.name: Get Remote Video Mute Lock Status

variable.description: The conference ID to check.

request.description:
This API provides the status of whether the video hidden with a mute lock is applied to a specified conference.
Output: `{ “result”: true }` (true if mute lock applied)
Codes: 200 OK, 404 Unavailable
